tree.setup: The pharmacy counter at the back of a small hospital, where afternoon light filters through frosted glass. Pharmacist Sun has been filling prescriptions for twenty years and reads doctors' handwriting the way archaeologists read ancient scripts — with patience, expertise, and occasional disbelief. He arranges pill bottles with the precision of a chess player setting up a board.
tree.setup_zh: 小医院后面的药房柜台，午后的光线透过磨砂玻璃照进来。药剂师孙师傅配药二十年，读医生的字迹就像考古学家读古文——需要耐心、专业知识，偶尔还需要一点信仰。他摆放药瓶的精确程度堪比棋手布局。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 您好，请把处方给我看看。
turns.text_pinyin: nín hǎo, qǐng bǎ chǔfāng gěi wǒ kànkan.
turns.text_en: Hello, please let me see your prescription.

options.text_zh: 给你，这是医生开的处方。请问这些药怎么吃？
options.text_pinyin: gěi nǐ, zhè shì yīshēng kāi de chǔfāng. qǐngwèn zhèxiē yào zěnme chī?
options.text_en: Here — this is the prescription the doctor gave me. How should I take these medicines?
options.feedback: Proactive about dosage instructions. Pharmacist Sun adjusts his reading glasses with satisfaction.
He takes it and squints at the handwriting. Some things never change.

options.text_zh: 我不知道医生写的什么，你能看懂吗？
options.text_pinyin: wǒ bù zhīdào yīshēng xiě de shénme, nǐ néng kàn dǒng ma?
options.text_en: I don't know what the doctor wrote — can you read it?
options.feedback: Pharmacist Sun chuckles.「放心，这个字迹我已经认了十五年了。」He's been reading this doctor's handwriting for fifteen years.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 好的，一共三种药。这个白色的是消炎药，一天三次，饭后吃。这个黄色的是止咳药，咳嗽的时候吃。
turns.text_pinyin: hǎo de, yígòng sān zhǒng yào. zhège báisè de shì xiāoyán yào, yì tiān sān cì, fàn hòu chī. zhège huángsè de shì zhǐ ké yào, késou de shíhòu chī.
turns.text_en: OK, three types of medicine. The white one is anti-inflammatory — three times a day, after meals. The yellow one is cough medicine — take it when you cough.

options.text_zh: 明白了。那第三种药是什么？也是饭后吃吗？
options.text_pinyin: míngbai le. nà dì sān zhǒng yào shì shénme? yě shì fàn hòu chī ma?
options.text_en: Got it. What's the third one? Also after meals?
options.feedback: Tracking all three — Sun nods approvingly. Most patients zone out after the second medication.
Short but on point. He pulls out the third bottle.
Sun smiles.「三种不算多。上星期有人开了八种。」Three is nothing — last week someone got eight.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 第三种是维生素C，每天一片，什么时候吃都行。对了，吃药期间不要喝酒。
turns.text_pinyin: dì sān zhǒng shì wéishēngsù C, měi tiān yí piàn, shénme shíhòu chī dōu xíng. duìle, chī yào qījiān búyào hē jiǔ.
turns.text_en: The third is vitamin C — one tablet daily, anytime is fine. Oh, and don't drink alcohol while on the medication.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Confirm you understand and ask about duration.

options.text_zh: 好的，不喝酒。请问这些药要吃几天？
options.text_pinyin: hǎo de, bù hē jiǔ. qǐngwèn zhèxiē yào yào chī jǐ tiān?
options.text_en: OK, no alcohol. How many days should I take these?
options.feedback: Duration matters — finishing antibiotics too early is Pharmacist Sun's recurring nightmare.
He'll tell you the duration anyway — he never lets a patient leave without knowing.

options.text_pinyin: yìdiǎn jiǔ dōu bù xíng ma?
options.feedback: Sun fixes you with a look that has ended a thousand negotiations.「一点都不行。」

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 消炎药吃五天，吃完为止，不要提前停。一共八十五块。
turns.text_pinyin: xiāoyán yào chī wǔ tiān, chī wán wéizhǐ, búyào tíqián tíng. yígòng bāshíwǔ kuài.
turns.text_en: Take the anti-inflammatory for five days — finish the course, don't stop early. That's 85 yuan total.

options.text_zh: 好的，五天吃完。给你八十五。谢谢孙师傅，解释得很清楚！
options.text_pinyin: hǎo de, wǔ tiān chī wán. gěi nǐ bāshíwǔ. xièxie Sūn shīfu, jiěshì de hěn qīngchǔ!
options.text_en: OK, five full days. Here's 85. Thanks, Pharmacist Sun — your explanation was very clear!
options.feedback: Confirming the course length and complimenting his clarity — Sun arranges the medicine bag for you with visible pride.
He hands you the bag with a nod. Another prescription filled, another patient sent on their way.
Sun pauses. Eighty-five yuan for three medications and twenty years of expertise. He lets the silence answer.

tree.cultural_note: In Chinese hospitals, the pharmacy (药房, yàofáng) is usually a separate window where you pick up prescriptions after seeing the doctor. Pharmacists are addressed as 药剂师 (yàojìshī) formally, or 师傅 (shīfu) casually, showing respect for their craft.
